Notes: Ad-hoc UAT for the Set 048 Lightweight-tier parity surface. Items verified programmatically in S5 by the AI orchestrator are marked Passes=true with Feedback documenting the verification path (unit test, smoke test, file inspection, etc.). Items requiring live VS Code interaction (right-click menu, wizard radio button, left-click toast) are flagged in Feedback as 'Manual operator UAT' and left Passes=false / Result=pending for the operator to walk through post-publish. The S5 UAT pass also discovered + fixed a Critical bare-import bug in production code; that fix is the last item below.

Review.ItemLabel: CLI flag wins over env var
Review.HumanAction: From a workspace with a spec where tier='full', run `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir <set> --session-number 1 --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort max --no-router` with `DABBLER_NO_ROUTER=` unset (or set to a value that contradicts the flag). Observe the log output and the orchestrator block written.
Review.Expectation: start_session prints a `log.info` line naming the CLI flag as the winning source when CLI/env/spec disagree. No live LLM call is attempted. Exit 0.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_runtime_mode.py::test_cli_overrides_spec_full_logs_info` (115 Set-048 tests pass).

Review.ItemLabel: spec.md tier=lightweight activates without CLI flag
Review.HumanAction: Author a spec with `tier: lightweight` in the canonical YAML block. Run `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir <set> --session-number 1 ...` with NO `--no-router` flag and `DABBLER_NO_ROUTER` unset.
Review.Expectation: Runtime mode resolves to no-router from the spec tier field alone. No live LLM call is attempted. start_session completes successfully.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_runtime_mode.py::test_spec_tier_lightweight_enables_no_router` + `test_spec_lightweight_alone_logs_info`.

Review.HumanAction: Author a spec whose CANONICAL yaml block has no `tier:` key, but the body markdown contains a sentence like 'This is the lightweight tier; tier: lightweight'.
Review.Expectation: parse_session_set_config returns tier='full' (default). Free-form prose can NEVER silently activate Lightweight mode. Regression test `test_tier_from_free_form_prose_is_ignored` covers this.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_spec_config.py::test_tier_from_free_form_prose_is_ignored` (S2 Round-A Important #5 in-flight fix).

Review.ItemLabel: Log info line names winning source
Review.HumanAction: Run start_session with `--no-router` against a spec that declares `tier: full`. Observe stderr/stdout.
Review.Expectation: log.info emits a line like 'CLI flag --no-router overrides spec tier `full` for this invocation.'
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_runtime_mode.py::test_cli_overrides_spec_full_logs_info` and `test_env_overrides_spec_full_logs_info`. S5 fix: logger name updated from bare `runtime_mode` to `ai_router.runtime_mode` to match the relative-import path.

Review.HumanAction: From a Python shell with DABBLER_NO_ROUTER=1, import ai_router and call route(prompt='hello', task_type='generic'). No API keys configured.
Review.Expectation: Returns a RouteResult with cost_usd=0, model='no-router', and a stub response. No live LLM call; no credential error.
Review.Feedback: S5 smoke test from CLI with simulated production sys.path (no conftest shim) confirmed RouteResult(cost_usd=0.0, model_name='no-router-mode') after the bare-import bug fix.

Review.ItemLabel: Exception in runtime_mode propagates rather than promoting
Review.HumanAction: Monkey-patch `runtime_mode.is_no_router_mode` to raise `RuntimeError('boom')` and call `route()`.
Review.Expectation: The RuntimeError propagates. The call does NOT silently fall through to a live LLM (this is the S2 Round-A Critical #1 fix).
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_no_router_short_circuit.py::test_route_fail_closed_on_runtime_mode_exception` (S2 Round-A Critical #1 in-flight fix).

Review.HumanAction: From an interactive terminal with --no-router, attempt to close a session whose set directory has NO external-verification.md.
Review.Expectation: close_session emits a one-line warning naming the missing file and prompts `Continue closing session without verification artifact? [y/N]`. Answering N aborts close-out with result='aborted_at_soft_gate' and a `closeout_failed` event in session-events.jsonl. Answering Y proceeds normally.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish: requires an interactive terminal; CI/automation paths only exercise the non-TTY branch. The TTY branch is unit-tested with stdin-mock in `test_no_router_close_session.py::test_soft_gate_tty_aborts_on_no`.

Review.HumanAction: Invoke close_session under --no-router with stdin redirected from /dev/null (or NUL on Windows).
Review.Expectation: Warning is emitted to stderr. close-out proceeds without prompting (CI-compatible).
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_no_router_close_session.py::test_soft_gate_non_tty_proceeds_with_warning`.

Review.HumanAction: Invoke close_session under --no-router with --accept-suggestions in a TTY where external-verification.md is absent.
Review.Expectation: No prompt fires. Warning is emitted; close-out proceeds.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_no_router_close_session.py::test_accept_suggestions_bypasses_soft_gate_in_tty`.

Review.HumanAction: Author a spec with `requiresUAT: "suggested"` (quoted) and `requiresE2E: suggested` (unquoted) in the YAML block. Call `parse_session_set_config(spec_md_path)`.
Review.Expectation: Returned config has requiresUAT='suggested' and requiresE2E='suggested'. No error.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_spec_config.py::test_requiresUAT_suggested_quoted` and `test_requiresE2E_suggested_unquoted`.

Review.HumanAction: From the extension under `npm run test:unit`, the existing schema tests must pass.
Review.Expectation: All TS schema tests pass; the SessionSetConfig type widens requiresUAT/requiresE2E to `boolean | 'suggested'`. Tier defaults to 'full' for back-compat.
Review.Feedback: S5: `npm run test:unit` = 665 pass + 2 pre-existing failures unrelated to Set 048.

Review.HumanAction: Parse a pre-Set-048 spec (no `tier:` key) with either parser.
Review.Expectation: tier='full' is returned. No error.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_spec_config.py::test_tier_defaults_to_full_when_missing` and TS-side `fileSystem.test.ts::parseSessionSetConfig defaults tier to full`.

Review.ItemLabel: Prompts list relative paths, NEVER embed
Review.HumanAction: From the Command Palette, run `Dabbler: Copy Spec Review Prompt` with any session-set row context. Paste clipboard contents into a notes file.
Review.Expectation: Clipboard contains relative paths (forward-slash normalized) like `docs/session-sets/<slug>/spec.md` and review instructions. No file contents are embedded. L1 honored.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `copyPromptCommands.test.ts::buildSpecReviewPrompt produces path-reference, not content-embed`. Manual VS Code spot-check recommended post-publish to confirm the toast.

Review.HumanAction: On a set with ≥1 completed session, run `Dabbler: Copy Session Accomplishments Prompt`.
Review.Expectation: Clipboard includes `git log --oneline <prev-session-tag>..HEAD` and `git diff <prev-session-tag>..HEAD` (or equivalent) plus path references to activity-log.json + change-log.md (if present).
Review.Feedback: Covered by `copyPromptCommands.test.ts::buildSessionAccomplishmentsPrompt embeds git-range command`.

Review.HumanAction: On a set whose state is `in-progress` or `not-started`, attempt to invoke `Dabbler: Copy Set Accomplishments Prompt` from the right-click `Copy Eval ▸` submenu.
Review.Expectation: The item is either absent or disabled per the §3.2 enablement rule (`state === 'complete'`).
Review.Feedback: Covered by `actionRegistry.test.ts::copyEval entries gating-predicates` (set-accomplishments shown only when state==='complete').

Review.HumanAction: Author a `docs/review-criteria/spec.md` with custom bullets. Run `Dabbler: Copy Spec Review Prompt`. Then delete the file and run the command again.
Review.Expectation: With the file present, its content appears in the prompt's optional-criteria slot. With the file absent, the prompt falls back to default English instructions. §3.9 carve-out documented in module header.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `copyPromptCommands.test.ts::readReviewCriteria embed-when-present and fallback-when-absent`.

Review.HumanAction: Construct a session-set whose slug contains a backtick character (manually rename a folder). Trigger the L5 left-click on its row.
Review.Expectation: Clipboard payload `Start the next session of \`<slug>\`.` has the backtick replaced with single-quote so the markdown stays well-formed. S3 Round-A Important #3 fix.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `copyPromptCommands.test.ts::sanitizeSlugForPrompt replaces backticks` and `rowMenuHelpers.test.ts::planLeftClickActivation sanitizes slug`.

Review.ItemLabel: Opens spec.md AND copies start-next prompt
Review.HumanAction: Left-click a row whose set state is `in-progress` or `not-started`.
Review.Expectation: spec.md opens in an editor tab. Clipboard now contains `Start the next session of `<slug>`.`. A one-line info toast `Copied: Start the next session of <slug>` appears.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish — requires live VS Code session to observe the toast. The pure decision logic is unit-tested in `rowMenuHelpers.test.ts::planLeftClickActivation`.

Review.HumanAction: Left-click a row whose set state is `complete` or `cancelled`.
Review.Expectation: spec.md opens. No clipboard write. No toast. Unknown future state values also FAIL CLOSED (no clipboard) per S3 Round-A Important #4.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. Pure decision logic covered by `rowMenuHelpers.test.ts::planLeftClickActivation fails closed on unknown state`.

Review.HumanAction: Right-click a session-set row.
Review.Expectation: A native vscode QuickPick appears (no cursor-anchored HTML popup). Top-level items include `Open File ▸`, `Copy Eval ▸` chips when their categories have applicable actions, plus flat actions inline.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. Negative-invariant covered at Layer 3 by `context-menu-quickpick.spec.ts` (.context-menu* DOM never appears).

Review.HumanAction: From the top-level QuickPick, click `Open File ▸`.
Review.Expectation: Second-level QuickPick lists Spec / Activity Log / Change Log (disabled if file absent) / Session State. Selecting one opens that file. Selecting Escape returns to the top level.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. `actionRegistry.test.ts::openFile submenu locks 4 entries` asserts the L2-locked count.

Review.ItemLabel: `Copy Eval ▸` submenu lists all four prompts
Review.HumanAction: From the top-level QuickPick, click `Copy Eval ▸`.
Review.Expectation: Second-level QuickPick lists Evaluate Specification / Evaluate Most Recent Session / Evaluate Session Set / Start Next Session. Items respect the enablement predicates from §3.2 (e.g., 'Evaluate Session Set' disabled on non-complete rows; 'Start Next Session' disabled on terminal rows).
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. Enablement predicates unit-tested in `actionRegistry.test.ts::copyEval entries gating-predicates`.

Review.HumanAction: Open the QuickPick, then press Escape OR click outside.
Review.Expectation: QuickPick dismisses (native VS Code behavior). No custom close button needed. L4 satisfied as a free byproduct.
Review.Feedback: L4 satisfied as a free byproduct of VS Code's native QuickPick dismissal behavior — no custom code needed; behavior owned by VS Code core. Operator can spot-check post-publish.

Review.ItemLabel: Menu does NOT include 'Open AI Assignment'
Review.HumanAction: Inspect both top-level and `Open File ▸` submenu QuickPicks.
Review.Expectation: No 'Open AI Assignment' / 'AI Assignment' entry anywhere. The `dabblerSessionSets.openAiAssignment` command is fully removed from package.json, ActionRegistry, openFile.ts, and COMMAND_ALLOWLIST. L3 honored.
Review.Feedback: Grep confirms no `dabblerSessionSets.openAiAssignment` registration; only the `aiAssignmentPath` data field remains on SessionSet. Covered by `actionRegistry.test.ts::openAiAssignment fully removed (L3)` and `context-menu-quickpick.spec.ts::openAiAssignment data-command absent`.

Review.HumanAction: Stage a session-state.json containing `sessionLog: [...]` (great-psalms-scroll-font shape). Run `python -m ai_router.migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4 --set-dir <dir> --apply`.
Review.Expectation: Exit 0 with action='migrated'. The output state file uses `sessions` (canonical v4). A `session-state.lwbak.json` backup exists at the same path.
Review.Feedback: S5 CLI smoke test: staged a sessionLog[]-shaped state file, ran migrate_one_set(dry_run=False), got action='migrated', schemaVersion=4, sessions[] present, sessionLog absent, .lwbak.json created.

Review.HumanAction: Run the migrator twice in sequence against a canonical v4 state file.
Review.Expectation: Second run reports `ACTION_SKIPPED_V4`. No backup created on the no-op pass.
Review.Feedback: S5 CLI smoke test: second run returned action='skipped-v4' as expected.

Review.HumanAction: Run the migrator against a pre-v3 state file.
Review.Expectation: Exit reports `ACTION_SKIPPED_PRE_V3` and points the operator at `migrate_session_state` (the v2→v3 migrator).
Review.Feedback: S5 CLI smoke test: pre-v3 file returned action='skipped-pre-v3' as expected.

Review.ItemLabel: Backup writes parsed dict, not re-read source
Review.HumanAction: Run the unit-test `test_backup_uses_parsed_state_not_reread_from_disk`.
Review.Expectation: Test passes. S4 Round-A Medium #1 fix verified: apply-mode backup contains the parsed state, not a re-read that could race a concurrent edit.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `test_migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4.py::test_backup_uses_parsed_state_not_reread_from_disk` (S4 Round-A Medium #1 in-flight fix).

Review.HumanAction: Open a workspace containing exactly one session set. Run `Dabbler: Open External Verification Doc` from the Command Palette.
Review.Expectation: external-verification.md opens (or is created on first invocation) in an editor tab. No QuickPick.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. Single-set path is documented in `externalVerification.ts` header comment and skips the picker by construction.

Review.HumanAction: In a workspace with ≥2 session sets, run the command.
Review.Expectation: QuickPick lists the sets with name + state. Selecting one opens that set's external-verification.md (creating if absent).
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. Multi-set path is documented in `externalVerification.ts` header comment.

Review.ItemLabel: Three template files exist at docs/review-criteria/
Review.HumanAction: Inspect docs/review-criteria/{spec,session,set}.md in the repo.
Review.Expectation: All three files exist, each with a comment-header explaining edit semantics and repo-specific sample bullets.
Review.Feedback: S5 file-inspection: all three files present at `docs/review-criteria/spec.md`, `session.md`, `set.md`.

Review.ItemLabel: Choose adoption tier appears above prerequisites
Review.HumanAction: Run `Dabbler: Get Started` to open the wizard.
Review.Expectation:
New '

Choose adoption tier

' radio group appears above '

Prerequisites

'. Default selection is Full.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. HTML structure inspected in `webview/wizard.html`; default Full preserved per S4 commit D.

Review.ItemLabel: Switching to Lightweight hides API-spend callout + Configure AI Router button
Review.HumanAction: Select the Lightweight radio option.
Review.Expectation: API-spend cost-reality callout hides; `Configure AI Router` and `Show Cost Dashboard` buttons hide; new Lightweight-only prerequisite (path-aware review agent) and no-API-spend callout become visible. `Troubleshoot` button stays.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish. applyTierVisibility() toggle is unit-testable as DOM-manipulation but the visual confirmation requires live VS Code.

Review.ItemLabel: Switching back to Full restores the API-spend surface
Review.HumanAction: Select Lightweight, then select Full again.
Review.Expectation: All Full-only sections return to visible state without page reload (applyTierVisibility re-runs on every change).
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT post-publish.

Review.HumanAction: Open docs/cross-repo-lightweight-notice.md and skim for the four required sections (activation / copy-prompt + paste-back flow / agent-capability requirement / migrator recipe + tier-branch wizard).
Review.Expectation: File exists and follows the established cross-repo-*.md pattern. All four sections present.
Review.Feedback: S5 file-inspection: file exists, 6.8 KB, matches cross-repo-checkout-notice.md and cross-repo-harvest-notice.md style.

Review.HumanAction: Open docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md and locate Step 6.
Review.Expectation: A 'Lightweight tier — copyable review prompts replace routed verification' subsection exists with the 5-step copy / paste / paste-back / soft-gate flow and the path-aware-agent requirement.
Review.Feedback: S5 grep confirms `#### Lightweight tier — copyable review prompts replace routed verification` heading at line 1392.

Review.HumanAction: Open docs/planning/session-set-authoring-guide.md.
Review.Expectation: tier: full / lightweight semantics + tri-state requiresUAT/requiresE2E semantics documented with the upfront-positive-confirmation prompt language (NOT triple-redundancy reminders, per operator override).
Review.Feedback: S5 grep: line 141 documents `tier: full | lightweight (Set 048+; pre-Set-048 specs default to "full")`; line 187 documents `single upfront positive-confirmation prompt`.

Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m pytest` from the repo root.
Review.Expectation: All Python tests pass (no Set-048 regressions). Documented pre-existing skips are unchanged.
Review.Feedback: S5: 1011 passed + 1 skipped (1 new test for production-import invariant; 0 regressions).

Review.HumanAction: Run `cd tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration && npm run test:unit` from the repo root.
Review.Expectation: All TS unit tests pass with the 2 pre-existing failures unchanged (configEditor-foundation panel-lifecycle + notificationsSection rendering — both predate Set 048).
Review.Feedback: S5: 665 passing + 2 pre-existing failures unchanged.

Review.HumanAction: Run `cd tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration && npx vsce package` after version bumps land.
Review.Expectation: Produces `dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.23.0.vsix`. No errors. File size is in the expected range (close to 0.22.0 + Set 048 additions).
Review.Feedback: S5: `npx vsce package --allow-missing-repository` produced `dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.23.0.vsix` (23 files, 881.47 KB). Build artifact removed (publish path is GitHub Actions, not local).

Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m build` from the repo root after version bump.
Review.Expectation: dist/ contains `dabbler_ai_router-0.10.0.tar.gz` and `dabbler_ai_router-0.10.0-py3-none-any.whl`. No build errors.
Review.Feedback: Deferred to the GitHub Actions release workflow (`release.yml`) per `reference_publish_via_github_actions` — local build is not part of the release path. Tag push triggers `python -m build` in CI.

Review.ItemLabel: Lightweight start_session writes identical orchestrator block
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir <set> --session-number 1 --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort max --no-router` against a fresh Lightweight set. Inspect the orchestrator block in `session-state.json`.
Review.Expectation: Orchestrator block contains engine, provider, model, effort fields populated identically to a Full-tier start_session. The --no-router flag does NOT short-circuit the state-file writer.
Review.Feedback: Architectural invariant: `runtime_mode.resolve_no_router_mode` runs in `start_session.main()` BEFORE `run(args)`; the writer in `run()` is unconditional and shared with Full-tier. The --no-router flag only short-circuits `route()`/`verify()` calls (which start_session doesn't make), not the orchestrator-block writer. Verified by inspection during S5 (`start_session.py:843-861` -> `run()` -> `register_session_start` in `session_state.py`, none of which read the runtime_mode cache). S5 actually exercised this when registering this session itself (sessions[4].orchestrator was populated identically to S1-S4 above).

Review.ItemLabel: Lightweight close_session appends to completedSessions[] identically to Full
Review.HumanAction: Close a session under --no-router with --manual-verify or after providing external-verification.md. Inspect `completedSessions` in `session-state.json`.
Review.Expectation: `completedSessions[]` includes the closed session number, sorted unique. Identical writer behavior to Full-tier close_session (the only difference is the verification artifact source: external-verification.md content instead of a routed verification call).
Review.Feedback: Architectural invariant: --no-router short-circuits `verify()` (the routed call) but NOT `_flip_state_to_closed` (the state-file writer). The soft gate in S2 commit C runs AFTER `run_gate_checks` and BEFORE the state flip; on TTY-affirmative answer the writer proceeds unchanged. Verified by inspection (close_session.py:1644-1657 -> `run(args)` -> shared writer path). Covered indirectly by `test_no_router_close_session.py::test_no_router_records_method_manual` and `test_no_router_uses_stock_attestation_when_no_reason_file`, which both write state files end-to-end under --no-router and assert the writer ran.

Review.ItemLabel: Lightweight emits the same session-events.jsonl entries as Full
Review.HumanAction: Run a start_session + close_session pair under --no-router. Tail `session-events.jsonl` and compare entry shapes against a Full-tier set.
Review.Expectation: `work_started`, `closeout_requested`, `verification_completed` (method='manual'), and `closeout_succeeded` events are emitted with identical fields. Per session-state-schema.md, the writer surface is tier-agnostic.
Review.Feedback: Architectural invariant: the `session_events.append_event` writer runs in the unconditional code path. Verified by inspection of session-events.jsonl after S2 close-out (entry shapes for sessions 2-4 under --no-router-aware code match those of session 1). The aborted-on-soft-gate path adds a `closeout_failed` event in addition to (not in place of) the standard ledger entries — covered by `test_no_router_tty_abort_emits_closeout_failed_event`.

Review.HumanAction: Simulate pip-install consumer: launch a Python with sys.path filtered so `ai_router/` is NOT on it (only the package site-packages location). Set DABBLER_NO_ROUTER=1, then call `ai_router.route(content='hi')`.
Review.Expectation: Returns a RouteResult with cost_usd=0.0, model_name='no-router-mode'. NO `ModuleNotFoundError`. Prior to the S5 fix, this call raised `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'runtime_mode'` because `ai_router/__init__.py:317` used a bare `from runtime_mode import …` that only worked under the test conftest's sys.path shim.
Review.Feedback: S5 UAT-discovered Critical bug: bare imports of `runtime_mode` / `spec_config` in production code (`__init__.py`, `start_session.py`, `close_session.py`, `runtime_mode.py`) silently broke `--no-router` for every pip-installed Lightweight consumer. The `start_session.main()` / `close_session.run()` sites wrapped the bare import in `try/except: pass`, so --no-router was a no-op for the entire production CLI surface. Fixed by switching to relative imports (`from .runtime_mode import …`). New `test_production_imports.py::test_no_bare_imports_of_set048_modules_in_production_code` locks the invariant at code-review time. Smoke test confirmed RouteResult(cost_usd=0.0, model_name='no-router-mode') under simulated pip-install sys.path. The original S2 Round-A verifier flagged this as Major #2 and the finding was dismissed as a false positive on conftest grounds; that dismissal was wrong.
